brief_justification: Paper A's machinery is empirical predictive-model evaluation for time series (loss ratios, Mincer-Zarnowitz recalibration, Model Confidence Set forecast-comparison tests comparing foundation models against HAR econometric benchmarks), whereas Paper B's machinery is numerical analysis of a differential equation (an Olver asymptotic construction mapping the curved hyperspherical Bessel equation onto the flat spherical Bessel equation for high-precision line-of-sight integration). There is no role-for-role correspondence: statistical forecast benchmarking and asymptotic special-function approximation are distinct mechanisms, and the only overlaps (the words 'forecast/prediction' and incidental use of AI tools) are keyword-level, not shared machinery.
